pathways.eligibility: 12th Pass with Physics, Chemistry & Mathematics (for Army, 12th pass in any stream is acceptable). Age: 16.5 - 19.5 years.
pathways.selectionProcess: UPSC Written Exam -> 5-Day SSB Interview -> Medical Examination
pathways.training: 3 Years at NDA Khadakwasla (B.A/B.Sc/B.Tech degree) + 1 Year specialized training at IMA (Army), INA (Navy), or AFA (Air Force)
pathways.rank: Lieutenant (Army) / Sub Lieutenant (Navy) / Flying Officer (Air Force)

pathways.eligibility: Graduation (IMA/OTA: Any degree; INA: Engineering degree; AFA: Degree with Physics & Math at 12th or Engineering). Age: 19 - 25 years.
pathways.selectionProcess: UPSC Written Exam -> 5-Day SSB Interview -> Medical Examination
pathways.training: IMA Dehradun (1.5 Years), INA Ezhimala (approx. 1 Year), AFA Dundigal (1.5 Years), OTA Chennai (49 Weeks)
pathways.rank: Lieutenant (Army) / Sub Lieutenant (Navy) / Flying Officer (Air Force)

pathways.eligibility: Graduation with minimum 60% marks and Physics & Math at 12th level. Age: 20 - 24 years (Flying Branch), 20 - 26 years (Ground Duty).
pathways.selectionProcess: AFCAT Written Exam (plus EKT for Technical Branch) -> Air Force Selection Board (AFSB) Interview -> Medicals
pathways.training: 74 Weeks for Flying & Technical branches, 52 Weeks for Non-Technical branches at Air Force Academy Dundigal

pathways.eligibility: B.E/B.Tech degree with minimum 60% marks from a recognized university. Age: 19 - 24 years.
pathways.selectionProcess: INET Written Exam -> Naval Service Selection Board (NSB) Interview -> Medicals
pathways.training: Sub Lieutenant course at Indian Naval Academy Ezhimala

pathways.eligibility: 10+2 Passed with Physics, Chemistry & Mathematics with minimum 60% aggregate. Must clear JEE Main. Age: 16.5 - 19.5 years.
pathways.selectionProcess: Shortlisting based on JEE Main Rank -> 5-Day SSB Interview -> Medicals
pathways.training: 5 Years total (1 Year Basic Military Training at OTA Gaya + 4 Years Technical Training at Cadets Training Wings)

pathways.eligibility: Engineering degree (B.E/B.Tech) final year or passed in relevant streams. No written exam. Age: 20 - 27 years.
pathways.selectionProcess: Shortlisting based on Engineering aggregate marks -> Direct SSB Interview -> Medicals
pathways.training: 49 Weeks at Officers Training Academy (OTA) Chennai

pathways.eligibility: Graduation. Must be gainfully employed in a professional role/self-employed. Age: 18 - 42 years.
pathways.selectionProcess: Civil Services Written Exam (for TA) -> Preliminary Interview Board (PIB) -> SSB Interview -> Medical Board
pathways.training: 1 Month Basic Training in first year, 2 months Annual Training Camp in subsequent years

pathways.scheme: CAPF (Central Armed Police Forces - Assistant Commandant)
pathways.eligibility: Graduation in any discipline. Age: 20 - 25 years.
pathways.selectionProcess: UPSC Written Exam -> Physical Efficiency Test (PET) & Medicals -> UPSC Personality Test/Interview
pathways.training: 52 Weeks basic training at respective force academies (BSF, CRPF, CISF, ITBP, SSB)
pathways.rank: Assistant Commandant (equivalent to Captain in Army / DSP in Police)

day1.title: Stage I: Screening
day1.details: Includes the Officer Intelligence Rating (OIR) test (verbal and non-verbal reasoning sheets) and the Picture Perception & Discussion Test (PPDT). Candidates write a story based on a hazy picture, narrate it individually, and discuss it in a group to reach a consensus story. Results are declared on the same day, and screened-out candidates return home.

day2.title: Stage II: Psychological Testing
day2.details: Conducted by a Psychologist in a classroom setting. It includes the Thematic Apperception Test (TAT) (writing stories for 11 pictures + 1 blank slide), Word Association Test (WAT) (writing immediate thoughts for 60 words shown), Situation Reaction Test (SRT) (writing reactions to 60 real-life crisis situations), and Self-Description Test (SDT) (writing opinions of parents, teachers, friends, and self).

day3.title: Stage II: Group Testing Officer (GTO) Tasks - Part 1
day3.details: Outdoor tasks testing team skills and leadership under stress. Includes Group Discussions (GD) on social/current issues, Group Planning Exercise (GPE) (finding a logical solution to a multi-crisis scenario map), Progressive Group Task (PGT) (crossing obstacles using ropes, planks, and structures as a team), Half Group Task (HGT), and Group Obstacle Race (Snake Race).

day4.title: Stage II: GTO Tasks - Part 2 & Personal Interview
day4.details: Includes the Lecturette (speaking on a selected topic for 3 minutes), Individual Obstacles (running through 10 athletic obstacles), Command Task (leading two subordinates to cross an obstacle), and Final Group Task. Concurrently, candidates undergo a 45-60 minute Personal Interview with the President/Deputy President of the Board regarding career, family, hobbies, and general knowledge.

day5.title: Stage II: Conference & Results
day5.details: All assessors (President, Psychologist, GTO, Interviewing Officer) sit in a uniform conference. The candidate is called in briefly for general feedback questions. The assessors pool their findings to make a final recommendation. The final list of recommended candidates is announced immediately, and they stay back for medical evaluation.
